risk_description: LLM hallucination or inconsistent JSON structure during AI SOW extraction (WI-AI-003) resulting in corrupted Project Brief data and failed vendor matching.
root_cause: Probabilistic nature of OpenAI models and variability in input SOW document formats.
mitigation_strategy: Implement rigorous Zod schema validation for LLM outputs with auto-retry logic on format failures. Enforce mandatory Human-in-the-Loop review (WI-AI-004) before data is persisted as 'Truth'.
contingency_plan: Fallback to manual data entry mode if AI service reliability degrades below 90%.
monitoring_trigger: Schema validation failure rate > 5% in extraction logs.

risk_description: Failure of AWS Comprehend PII redaction (WI-AI-002) allowing sensitive client data to leak into vector embeddings or vendor-facing views, violating GDPR/SOC 2 requirements.
root_cause: Automated NLP models achieving less than 100% recall on diverse document types.
mitigation_strategy: Implement multi-layer sanitization: AWS Comprehend + Regex pattern matching. Store original documents in a strictly isolated, limited-access S3 bucket with lifecycle policies.
contingency_plan: Immediate purge procedure for contaminated embeddings and notification workflow for affected clients.
monitoring_trigger: Detection of PII patterns in the 'Sanitized' S3 bucket via AWS Macie scans.

risk_description: Financial ledger desynchronization due to missed or duplicate Stripe webhooks (WI-FIN-001), leading to incorrect project statuses (Active/Awarded) and revenue discrepancies.
root_cause: Network failures, lack of idempotency, or race conditions in webhook processing logic.
mitigation_strategy: Implement robust idempotency guards using Redis to track `event_id`. Use Stripe Signature verification. Create a daily reconciliation cron job comparing Stripe balance vs. internal ledger.
contingency_plan: Manual reconciliation admin tool to force-update transaction status based on Stripe Dashboard data.
monitoring_trigger: Unprocessed webhook DLQ depth > 0 or mismatched ledger sums in nightly report.

risk_description: pgvector search latency exceeding the 250ms p95 requirement as the vendor/project embedding dataset scales, degrading user experience.
root_cause: Inefficient indexing strategy (IVFFlat vs HNSW) or lack of read replicas for high-load queries.
mitigation_strategy: Utilize HNSW indexes for vector columns. Implement caching layer (Redis) for frequent search queries. Provision read replicas for the RDS Postgres instance.
contingency_plan: Migrate vector workload to a dedicated vector database (e.g., Pinecone/Weaviate) if Postgres vertical scaling hits limits.
monitoring_trigger: Average DB query time for vector search > 150ms.

risk_description: OpenAI API rate limiting or latency spikes causing SOW processing timeouts and failure of the async pipeline (WS-AI-PIPELINE).
root_cause: Dependency on external 3rd party API limits and shared infrastructure availability.
mitigation_strategy: Implement exponential backoff retry logic in SQS consumers. Request quota increase from OpenAI. Optimize prompt token usage to reduce load.
contingency_plan: Queue system automatically creates 'Pending' status allowing users to return later rather than failing the request UI.

risk_description: Complexity of distributed transactions (Saga Pattern) causing data inconsistency between Project, Payment, and AI microservices during failure scenarios.
root_cause: Microservices architecture lacking strict transactional boundaries across service domains.
mitigation_strategy: Define explicit compensation events for every transaction step. Use a persistent event bus (SNS/SQS) with DLQ for message durability.
contingency_plan: Implement 'Event Replay' capability to restore state from the immutable audit log.
monitoring_trigger: Correlation ID analysis shows 'Started' but missing 'Completed' events for > 1% of flows.
